_meta.description: Curated source bank for Animal Facts Song Short. This is not a narrative story bank. It is a songability-first topic bank optimized for immediate animal naming, strong hook traits, escalating fact wonder, fast visual readability, and loop ending potential.
_meta.selection_rule: Prefer animals with strong immediate naming value, high visual distinctiveness, 3-5 singable fact beats, and a natural loop-back ending idea.

scoring_notes.kidFamiliarityScore: 0-1, how likely a child/parent pair already recognizes or quickly accepts the animal
scoring_notes.visualDistinctivenessScore: 0-1, how visually memorable the silhouette/features are in under 3 seconds
scoring_notes.surpriseScore: 0-1, how naturally surprising the animal's best facts are
scoring_notes.cutenessScore: 0-1, cuteness/endearment value for replay and saves
scoring_notes.songabilityScore: 0-1, how easily the animal/facts convert into catchy lyric beats
scoring_notes.sceneVarietyScore: 0-1, how many visually different beat scenes the animal can support

topics.signatureHookTrait: tiny fox with giant ears that help it survive desert heat
factBeats: Its ears are huge compared with its tiny body.
Those giant ears help release body heat in the hot desert.
It can hear prey moving under the sand.
Its furry feet help protect it from burning ground.
It pops, listens, and pounces in quick playful motions.
visualSetPieces: tiny fox with oversized ears under a glowing desert sky
close shot of translucent ears catching warm light
fox listening with head tilted over rippled sand
springy pounce into sand
small paw prints and fluffy feet on sunlit dunes
topics.loopEndingIdea: end on the fox listening with giant ears again so the first hook line can restart naturally
avoidNotes: do not let the visuals drift into generic red-fox forest imagery
avoid crowded desert scenes that hide the ear silhouette

topics.signatureHookTrait: a rainforest animal that looks part zebra, part deer, but is its own thing
factBeats: It looks striped like a zebra but is closer to a giraffe.
Its long dark tongue can grab leaves and clean its own eyes and ears.
Its striped legs help it vanish into broken forest light.
It walks quietly through dense rainforest shadows.
Its look is so unusual that every beat can reveal a new surprise.
visualSetPieces: full-body okapi reveal in dappled jungle light
close-up of striped legs in layered foliage shadows
tongue stretching toward leaves
quiet walking pose through narrow jungle path
half-hidden silhouette blending with shafts of light
topics.loopEndingIdea: end on the strange stripes and ask visually what kind of animal it even is, so the opening naming line feels replayable
avoidNotes: do not make it read as a zebra in a jungle
keep giraffe-family anatomy accurate in the face and body

topics.signatureHookTrait: a funny seabird with a bright beak that can carry several fish at once
factBeats: Its beak glows with bright colors in breeding season.
It can hold multiple fish crosswise at the same time.
It flies fast but looks adorably frantic in the air.
It nests in burrows on steep grassy cliffs.
It dives underwater to chase fish with its wings.
visualSetPieces: bright-beaked puffin perched on a cliff edge
beak full of fish in side profile
puffin flapping low over waves
burrow opening in grassy cliff wall
underwater-style dive motion beat
topics.loopEndingIdea: end on the fish-filled beak so the opener about the funny bright-beaked bird can restart cleanly
avoidNotes: do not let it drift into penguin-like body staging
keep the beak shape oversized and iconic

topics.signatureHookTrait: a little armored mammal with a shell-like body and digging claws
factBeats: Its body is covered in armor-like plates.
It digs quickly with strong curved claws.
Some armadillos can curl tighter when frightened.
It sniffs along the ground for insects.
Its funny shuffle and armor shape make it instantly memorable.
visualSetPieces: armadillo profile showing plate bands
digging action with dirt flying
nose-down insect search pose
curled protective posture
little armored shuffle at golden hour
topics.loopEndingIdea: end on the rolling/armor idea so the first naming line about the armored animal can replay naturally
avoidNotes: avoid making the shell look like turtle anatomy
do not overcrowd scenes with too much vegetation

topics.signatureHookTrait: a tiny spiky nighttime explorer with a surprisingly busy nose
factBeats: Its back is covered with sharp protective spines.
It sniffs and rustles around mostly at night.
It can curl into a ball when danger comes close.
It hunts little bugs with patient searching.
Its tiny feet and nose make every movement feel cute and rhythmic.
visualSetPieces: small hedgehog under moonlit garden glow
close-up of spines catching light
curled defensive ball pose
nose-down insect search in leaves
tiny walking silhouette through grass
topics.loopEndingIdea: end with the hedgehog rustling into the night so the opening line can start again like a bedtime loop
avoidNotes: keep the night scenes readable, not muddy

topics.signatureHookTrait: a fluffy ocean animal that floats, cracks shells, and wraps in kelp
factBeats: It floats on its back like a tiny drifting raft.
It uses rocks as tools to crack open shellfish.
It wraps itself in kelp so it does not drift away.
Its fur is extremely dense and helps keep it warm.
Its paws, whiskers, and floating pose are instantly lovable on screen.
visualSetPieces: otter floating on back in blue-green water
shell cracking with rock on chest
kelp wrapping scene with playful motion
close-up whiskers and paws
gentle wave bobbing for loop ending
topics.loopEndingIdea: end with the otter bobbing on its back so the opening line can loop like a floating lullaby
avoidNotes: keep kelp and shell-tool behavior present in the visual language
